query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "leveraging verifier-based reinforcement learning in image editing" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on Bayesian Inverse Reinforcement Learning (BIRL) for modeling human planning hierarchies, which is a distinct methodological construct from the user's specific interest in "Verifier-Based Reinforcement Learning" for "Image Editing." While both involve reinforcement learning, the paper does not measure the same mechanism (verifier-based reward shaping), operate in the same domain (image generation/editing), or provide a canonical baseline for the specific verifier-based approac
